plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-235 RP07.P00.M00.S01-RP07.P01.M02.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-235 bootstraps the RP07 Search OCR And Index program with a new @law-firm-os/search descriptor package, search-core contract, and rp07:search-core:validate gate, implementing descriptor-only coverage of the full RP07.P00 Scope And Contract phase (eleven micros) and the RP07.P01 Model And Storage Foundation head (three micros) as fourteen descriptor sections over 150 planned rows, including scope inventories, H07/C07 acceptance gates, non-goal boundaries (no search/OCR/index/embedding runtime), target file maps, baseline notes, synthetic data policies, risk registers, blocked-claim rules, Hermes preflight fields, Claude review prompts, human approval notes, dependency lists, command matrices, receipt shapes, package layout, entity identifiers, tenant scope fields, matter trace references, lifecycle enums, and field registries, with Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and CP00-236 handoff, without opening search runtime, OCR runtime, index runtime, embedding runtime, permission runtime evaluation, audit event writes, state writes, object storage, email, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-235 is a descriptor-only RP07 (Search OCR And Index) scope-and-contract foundation pack covering 150 planned units RP07.P00.M00.S01-RP07.P01.M02.S08. Independent read-only inspection supports the pack's claims and finds no blocking issues. Coverage was recomputed against the authoritative plan (docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json:412, present with identical unit_count 150, range, and source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65), so validation is not circular; all six deliverable counts (implementation 108, contract 8, security_audit 16, hermes_evidence 7, claude_review 3, ui 8 = 150), all 11 micro-title counts, and all 14 micro-phase counts reconcile across registry.js, the contract JSON, the baseline, and the plan. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are verified at the code level: packages/search/src contains no writeFile/child_process/fetch/eval/process.env, only frozen object construction, and validators.js actively enforces descriptor_only, no search/OCR/index/embedding runtime dispatch, deny-over-allow, no cross-tenant access, no real-data loading, no audit-body/raw-payload leakage, human approval required before runtime opening, Claude-not-final-approver, and no enterprise-trust claim from local validation. The contract embeds the generated descriptor and historical binding (tail pack_id CP00-235) and is deepEqual-validated by the validator and test (command-evidence exit 0). Authority boundary is correctly maintained: pack-level production_ready is false (conservative; status implemented_pending_review), manifest sets claude_not_final_approver and enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation true, Hermes patterns are descriptor-only with no runtime receipts emitted, and review-gated commands (closeout-pack:validate etc.) are correctly deferred to post-review. CP178+ readiness holds: the auth preflight produced valid JSON with non-zero stdout (1455 bytes); no auth-failure, 0-byte, malformed-JSON, fenced-JSON-with-prose, or tool-call-shaped evidence is relied upon. One non-blocking P3 observation about a currently-inert manifest-snapshot fallback in the validator/test. Claude is acting as read-only reviewer only; final human approval for any enterprise trust claim remains required and is out of scope of this receipt.

findings.title: Coverage validator/test fall back to pack-authored manifest plan_binding_snapshot when plan entry is absent
findings.details: Both the validator (scripts/validate-rp07-search-core-contract.mjs:35) and the unit test (packages/search/test/model.test.js:28) select the coverage source as `closeoutPlan.packs.find((pack) => pack.pack_id === 'CP00-235') ?? cp235Manifest?.plan_binding_snapshot`. If the authoritative CP00-235 entry were ever removed or renamed in docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json, coverage validation would silently fall back to the pack-authored snapshot embedded in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-235/manifest.json (line 17), which slightly reduces the independence of the coverage gate. This is currently inert: CP00-235 is present in the plan at closeout-pack-plan.json:412 with content identical to the manifest snapshot (same source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65e83631946ea7fae75c4d53a6429b2b41576fe099027dfaffda91fa32, unit_count 150, range RP07.P00.M00.S01-RP07.P01.M02.S08), so the primary plan path is used and the snapshot is only a faithful defensive copy. No coverage drift or self-satisfaction is occurring.
findings.recommendation: Optionally harden by asserting the plan contains the CP00-235 entry (fail hard) instead of falling back to the manifest snapshot, so coverage can never be satisfied solely from pack-authored data. Non-blocking; no action required for this closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational note. Does not block pack closeout or goal closeout; the authoritative plan entry is present and identical, so the fallback path is not exercised.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-235 search core scope contract foundation descriptor pack
